query: What is the current status of the 2026 Tour de France as of 13 July 2026? Latest general classification standings, has Tadej Pogačar retained the yellow jersey, any crashes or abandonments, current gap to Vingegaard.
answer:
I've completed my research on the 2026 Tour de France status as of 13 July 2026. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Bottom line:** As of 13 July 2026, the Tour is on its **first rest day** (in Cantal), so standings reflect the situation after Stage 9.

- **Pogačar has retained the yellow jersey** — he took it on Stage 6 over the Col du Tourmalet and has held it comfortably through the sprint/breakaway stages 7–9.
- **Gap to Vingegaard: exactly 2:42** — unchanged since Stage 6, as the GC contenders finished together in the intervening stages. Vingegaard remains his closest challenger in 2nd.
- **Top 5 GC:** Pogačar (yellow), Vingegaard +2:42, Del Toro +3:27 (white jersey), Evenepoel +3:30, Ayuso +3:34.
- **Crashes/abandonments:** No GC-defining crash affected the top two favorites. Notable exits include former leader Torstein Træen (crashed on Stage 6, concussion + rib fractures) and GC hopeful Cian Uijtdebroeks (illness, abandoned on the Tourmalet). Multiple Stage 1 TTT crashes affected Groupama-FDJ and XDS Astana.
- **Other context:** Stage 9 was shortened due to a heatwave; Mathieu van der Poel won it; Merlier won Stages 7 & 8; Pedersen leads the green jersey.

Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 GC standings – Pogačar remains in yellow
sources.snippet: Pogačar leads Vingegaard by 2:42; Del Toro 3rd at 3:27, Evenepoel 4th at 3:30, Ayuso 5th at 3:34 after Stage 9. Rest day precedes Stage 10 (Aurillac–Le Lioran).

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Classifications Update Stage 9
sources.snippet: GC after Stage 9: Pogačar 32:17:04, Vingegaard +2:42 (unchanged), Del Toro +3:27, Evenepoel +3:30, Ayuso +3:34. Carapaz 23rd (+15:24).

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 rest days: when they happen and why they matter
sources.snippet: The first rest day comes on Monday 13 July in Cantal, after the Pyrenees.

sources.title: Tour de France abandons – All the riders who have quit the 2026 race
sources.snippet: Berthet (DNS St2), De Lie (DNF St3), O'Brien (OTL St4), Molenaar (DNS St6), Van Lerberghe, de Kleijn, Uijtdebroeks (St6), Træen (DNS St7 after St6 crash).

sources.title: Tour de France 2026: All you need to know
sources.snippet: Pogačar took control on Stage 6 with an attack near the summit of the Col du Tourmalet, set up by Del Toro, riding away from Vingegaard.

sources.title: Torstein Træen withdraws from Tour de France 2026 after stage 6 crash
sources.snippet: Uno-X confirmed Træen will not start stage 7 after a concussion and multiple rib fractures from a stage 6 crash.

sources.title: 2026 Tour de France
sources.snippet: On Stage 4 Torstein Træen took yellow in a large breakaway where Pedersen won the stage. Pogačar returned to yellow on Stage 6 over the Tourmalet.

sources.title: Merlier cruises to victory on Stage 7
sources.snippet: Tim Merlier (Soudal Quick-Step) won Stage 7 in Bordeaux ahead of Philipsen and Waerenskjold.

sources.title: How to watch Tour de France Stage 9
sources.snippet: Stage 9 shortened from 185.1 km to 155.5 km due to an intense heatwave in the Corrèze region.

sources.title: Two Massive Crashes Rock Tour de France Team Time Trial
sources.snippet: Groupama-FDJ and XDS Astana riders crashed at the same manhole cover during the opening TTT.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026: Withdrawals
sources.snippet: Clément Berthet was the first abandon, crashing in the opening TTT on a manhole cover, DNS stage 2.

key_facts: 13 July 2026 is the first rest day of the Tour de France (Monday, in Cantal); racing is paused, and current standings are those after Stage 9.
Tadej Pogačar (UAE Team Emirates-XRG) has retained the yellow jersey; he took it on Stage 6 over the Col du Tourmalet.
Pogačar leads Jonas Vingegaard (Visma-Lease a Bike) by 2 minutes 42 seconds in the general classification.
The Pogačar–Vingegaard gap of 2:42 has remained unchanged from Stage 6 through Stage 9 (Stages 7-9 were sprint/breakaway stages).
Isaac del Toro (UAE) is 3rd overall at +3:27 and holds the white young-rider jersey; Remco Evenepoel 4th (+3:30), Juan Ayuso 5th (+3:34).
Stage 9 (Malemort–Ussel) was won by Mathieu van der Poel; it was shortened from 185.1 km to 155.5 km due to a heatwave.
Former yellow-jersey holder Torstein Træen (Uno-X Mobility) crashed on Stage 6 and abandoned with a concussion and multiple rib fractures.
GC hopeful Cian Uijtdebroeks (Movistar) abandoned on the Col du Tourmalet on Stage 6 due to illness.
No crash has affected the two main GC favorites (Pogačar or Vingegaard) so far.
The Tour began 4 July 2026 in Barcelona (TTT) with 184 riders; ~177 remained after Stage 6. It finishes in Paris on 26 July.
Tim Merlier (Soudal-QuickStep) won Stages 7 and 8; Mads Pedersen (Lidl-Trek) leads the green points jersey.
